element_introduction_order.mastery_context: central defensive objective - destruction means loss
basic labor for digging, mining, capturing portals and rooms
picking up creatures/objects, slapping creatures, interaction mechanism
imp labor task - expanding dungeon territory
resource acquisition for building and spells
necessary rooms for creature housing and sustenance
enables minion recruitment - introduces first allied creatures
combat creatures with varied abilities and prerequisites
training room progression - stat and spell advancement
unlocking new rooms and spells over campaign
defensive mechanisms - lightning traps, boulder traps, doors
morale management and risk/reward deity interactions
invading forces seeking treasure and dungeon heart destruction
competing keepers in same realm - scavenging and conquest targets
angry creatures vandalize/desert; payday wages required
creature happiness management - regular treasury drain
ultimate heroic threat - resembles Ultima VIII Avatar

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: escalating campaign with early safe building phase, mid-game introduction of combat pressure and rival competition, late-game climactic hero and keeper confrontations

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: creature prerequisites and room availability define accessible minion types and strategic options
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: tile ownership color-coding (player red, rivals different colors, heroes white, unaligned multicolor) communicates territorial control and allegiance at a glance

difficulty_curve.early_game: tutorial-like infrastructure focus with safe dungeon building and basic imp labor management
difficulty_curve.mid_game: increasing complexity from technology tree progression, creature recruitment prerequisites, morale management, and introduction of defensive trap systems
difficulty_curve.late_game: sustained pressure from hero invasions, rival keeper competition for world conquest, escalating creature army requirements, and climactic Avatar encounter
notable_spikes: first portal connection and minion arrival
initial hero invasion requiring trap/creature defense
rival keeper emergence in multi-keeper realms
Avatar appearance signaling final campaign phase

notable_level_patterns.description: early levels focus on digging, gold mining, and building essential rooms (Lair, Hatchery, Treasury) before creature recruitment becomes viable
transition phase where connecting dungeon to portal enables minion influx; creature prerequisites and room capacity gate available army composition
mid-game shift toward trap/door placement, creature training, and morale management in anticipation of hero invasion waves
realms with rival keepers introduce scavenging mechanics and territorial conquest alongside hero defense
final campaign phases intensify hero waves and introduce Avatar as ultimate heroic threat requiring maximal dungeon capability
